core_mechanism: strong linear (proportional) correlation between the equilibrium fluctuations of two conjugate observables, arising because a single scale-invariant homogeneous (inverse-power-law) interaction dominates those fluctuations and collapses their joint variation onto an effective one-parameter scaling relation
brief_reason: The abstract reports strong correlations between equilibrium fluctuations of the configurational parts of pressure and energy and attributes them to an effective inverse power-law potential dominating the fluctuations.
